faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Uniondale home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery; silken webbing or cocoons in closets and drawers; adult moths flying near wardrobes; and damage to natural fiber materials. You may also notice larvae (small cream-colored caterpillars) or shed skins in infested areas. Our Uniondale technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of the problem.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Uniondale?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550 and $5,000 depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of the affected area, the number of rooms or closets requiring treatment, and whether repeat visits are necessary. Larger homes with extensive fabric collections or severe infestations require more comprehensive treatment, while smaller, localized problems are more affordable to address.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take and when can I use my closets again?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 1-3 hours depending on the size of your Uniondale property and infestation severity. Most treatments allow you to return to treated areas within 2-4 hours after application once surfaces are dry. We'll provide specific instructions for your situation, including guidance on cleaning fabrics and preventing re-infestation.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths spread from one room to another in my Uniondale home?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths can easily spread throughout your home as adult moths fly to new locations and larvae can be transported on infested clothing or fabrics. This is why comprehensive treatment of all affected and adjacent areas is crucial. Our technicians inspect your entire Uniondale property to identify all potential infestation sites and prevent moths from relocating to untreated areas.
